# UC-10.12.4 · Account Takeover Indicators

## Description

Combines login, device, and session signals to catch ATO before fraudulent transfers.

## Value

Combines login, device, and session signals to catch ATO before fraudulent transfers.

## Implementation

Require device fingerprint and step-up auth on risk score. Correlate password reset + wire initiation within short windows.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: IdP logs, mobile banking SDK, SIEM correlation.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="okta:systemlog"`, `sourcetype="banking:session"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Require device fingerprint and step-up auth on risk score. Correlate password reset + wire initiation within short windows.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=identity OR index=banking (sourcetype="okta:systemlog" OR sourcetype="banking:session")
| search (eventType="user.session.start" OR event="new_device") OR (risk_score>70)
| transaction user_id maxspan=1h maxpause=10m
| where mvcount(device_id)>2 OR mvcount(geo_country)>2
| table _time, user_id, device_id, geo_country, risk_score
```

Understanding this SPL

**Account Takeover Indicators** — Combines login, device, and session signals to catch ATO before fraudulent transfers.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="okta:systemlog"`, `sourcetype="banking:session"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IdP logs, mobile banking SDK, SIEM correlation. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: identity, banking; **sourcetype**: okta:systemlog, banking:session. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=identity, index=banking, sourcetype="okta:systemlog". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvcount(device_id)>2 OR mvcount(geo_country)>2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Account Takeover Indicators**): table _time, user_id, device_id, geo_country, risk_score


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (user journey), Table (risky sessions), Sankey (device → action).

## SPL

```spl
index=identity OR index=banking (sourcetype="okta:systemlog" OR sourcetype="banking:session")
| search (eventType="user.session.start" OR event="new_device") OR (risk_score>70)
| transaction user_id maxspan=1h maxpause=10m
| where mvcount(device_id)>2 OR mvcount(geo_country)>2
| table _time, user_id, device_id, geo_country, risk_score
```
